soap:
REVIEW · lance-format/lance#6265 — feat: support hamming clustering
jackye1995 (core) · XL (+2848/-58, 13 files)  ⚠ escalation forced

ONE-LINER
  Adds SIMD-accelerated pairwise hamming distance + binary-vector clustering
  (union-find over a hash column), with new lance::index::vector::hamming entry
  points (IVF partition / sample / fragment range) and a Python binding surface
  (vector.py + pyo3 Dataset methods). Net +2848 lines, two ~1k-line new modules.

SUBJECTIVE  (author's framing)
  PR body: SIMD pairwise hamming + cluster binary vectors within a threshold to
  find near-duplicate representations; exposed in Python. Author is core; this is
  a first review (fresh lane, blocked_on=me, no prior ledger).

OBJECTIVE  (what I checked, tied to ground truth)
  CI / merge state
  • merge_state: ci=red, mergeable=false. Every gate is failing at head dca3410:
    format, clippy, rustdoc, MSRV, linux/mac/windows/arm builds, build-no-lock,
    query-integration-tests, aws-integtest, lint, Java, and all Python wheels.
    This is wholesale-red, not a flake — the branch does not build/lint as-is.

  Correctness — lance-linalg/src/distance/hamming.rs
  • L398-399 extract_hashes_from_fixed_list — reads values.values()[i*8 .. i*8+8]
    with i in 0..array.len(), with NO array.offset() term and NO null/validity
    check. A sliced FixedSizeListArray (non-zero offset) reads the wrong bytes and
    can over-read / panic; null list entries are silently treated as valid data.
    Confirmed against the patch. This function has ZERO test coverage.
  • L111/L168-198/L233-237 SIMD pairwise (avx512 store, avx2, scalar) rely on
    results.len()==targets.len() but enforce it only via debug_assert_eq! — in a
    release build a short `results` buffer is an out-of-bounds heap WRITE through
    _mm256_storeu_si256 / raw ptr. The unsafe blocks assume an invariant nothing
    guarantees in release. No test exercises a length mismatch.
  • L97/L567 .expect("Failed to create RecordBatch") panics on user-controlled
    data instead of returning Result (non-blocking).
  • UnionFind (L408-475) is correct: path compression + union-by-rank with a
    deterministic min-id tie-break; representative is chosen as cluster-min
    independent of union order (L638), so clustering output is order-independent
    (verified by test_cluster_edges_random_order). The parallel path concatenates
    per-chunk results in chunk order, so output is stable. Good.

  Correctness — lance/src/index/vector/hamming.rs (NEW)
  • L294-298 total_rows sums fragment physical_rows — counts DELETED rows. The
    sampling fallback threshold (sample_size < total_rows) and the sample index
    space are both computed against this inflated count. Untested on a dataset
    with deletions.
  • L304-319 hamming_clustering_for_sample builds positional indices in
    0..total_rows and feeds them to dataset.take(&[u64]). If take interprets the
    u64s as row addresses rather than dense positional offsets, sampled rows are
    wrong / take fails. The take-semantics assumption is unverified and untested.
  • L449-472 hamming_clustering_for_range applies limit/offset over a post-delete
    scan while bounds-checking against physical_rows (includes deletes) — same
    physical-vs-logical mismatch; the clamping path (num_rows past fragment end)
    is untested.
  • L164/L324/L330/L480/L487 as_fixed_size_list()/as_primitive downcasts on
    scan/storage results panic on type mismatch; the storage FLAT_COLUMN type is
    not re-validated before downcast (non-blocking).
  • Multi-fragment/multi-batch hash↔row_id alignment (IVF path L138/L157-167)
    assumes to_batches() ordering matches storage.row_ids() ordering — not
    asserted (question).

  Surface / API
  • Public-API + large-blast-radius escalation is FORCED → full rounds regardless
    of how clean the code looks. New pub fns in lance-linalg distance::* and a new
    public lance::index::vector::hamming module + 4 new pyo3 Dataset methods. The
    Python typing imports (Optional/List) are present in vector.py head — clean.
  • lance-linalg gains a rayon dependency; the old standalone hamming bench was
    deleted and re-added under rust/lance/benches.

ASSESSMENT
  risk: HIGH (refined up from baseline med) · urgency: high
  Rationale: refined to HIGH from the diff — an unguarded unsafe SIMD store
  (release-mode OOB write) and an offset/null-blind FixedSizeList reader, both on
  a brand-new public IO/compute path, both with zero direct test coverage, on top
  of physical-vs-logical row-count bugs in the dataset-reading helpers. These are
  IO-correctness + memory-safety, not style.
  escalation: forced on [public-api, large-blast-radius] → full rounds.
  could not read: none (13/13 files in packet; both ~1k-line hamming files read
  in full from GitHub, not the truncated packet copy).

DRAFT COMMENTS  (drafts only — never posted)
  issue(blocking): extract_hashes_from_fixed_list ignores array.offset() and
    nulls (lance-linalg/src/distance/hamming.rs:398) — acceptance: index the
    child values at (array.offset()+i)*8, handle/ reject null list entries, and
    add a test that passes a SLICED FixedSizeList<UInt8,8> (non-zero offset) and a
    null-containing array and asserts correct/erroring behavior.

  issue(blocking): SIMD pairwise relies on a debug-only length invariant
    (lance-linalg/src/distance/hamming.rs:111,168) — acceptance: enforce
    results.len()==targets.len() with a real assert!/length check (or bound the
    stores) so a release build cannot perform an out-of-bounds write; add a test
    covering a mismatched-length buffer.

  issue(blocking): sample/range row counting uses physical_rows incl. deletions
    (lance/src/index/vector/hamming.rs:294,449) — acceptance: compute the sample
    space and range bounds from the LOGICAL row count, and add a test over a
    dataset with deleted rows showing sampling/range never address deleted
    positions.

  issue(blocking): verify dataset.take semantics for sampled indices
    (lance/src/index/vector/hamming.rs:304) — acceptance: confirm take(&[u64])
    interprets these as positional offsets (cite the take contract) or switch to
    the correct addressing; add a multi-fragment sample test that asserts the
    returned hashes match the sampled rows.

  issue(blocking): CI is wholesale-red at head (format/clippy/rustdoc/builds/
    integration on all platforms) — acceptance: green CI, or at minimum cargo fmt
    + clippy clean and the Rust/Python build jobs passing.

  issue(non-blocking): replace .expect("Failed to create RecordBatch") and the
    bare downcasts (as_fixed_size_list / as_primitive) with Result-returning
    error paths so malformed input errors instead of panicking
    (lance-linalg .../hamming.rs:97,567; lance .../hamming.rs:164).

  question: in the IVF path, is to_batches() ordering guaranteed to match
    storage.row_ids() so hashes and row-ids stay index-aligned?
    (lance/src/index/vector/hamming.rs:157)

  praise: UnionFind + clustering is solid — deterministic min-representative
    independent of edge/union order, with thorough order-independence tests.

RECOMMENDATION: block on 5 conditions (4 code + green CI)
  confidence: high on the two memory/IO-safety blockers (extract offset/null +
  SIMD release store) — both verified against the patch and both untested. The
  deletion/ take-semantics blockers are high-confidence-as-questions: the bug
  exists if take(&[u64]) is positional and datasets can have deletions, which is
  the normal case.
  what would make this wrong: if a layer above always passes contiguous,
  offset-zero, non-null, exactly-length-matched arrays AND datasets in scope are
  guaranteed delete-free — then the offset/length/deletion concerns degrade from
  bugs to missing defensive guards. The SIMD release-store OOB and the absent
  tests still stand regardless.
